title: IGF-1
description: Insulin-like growth factor-1 (IGF-1) is a primary mediator of growth hormone (GH) that promotes cellular growth, differentiation, and anabolic effects in almost every tissue of the body. It plays a critical role in bone development and muscle hypertrophy by activating signaling pathways like mTOR and PI3K.

research.title: Supplementing With Which Form of Creatine (Hydrochloride or Monohydrate) Alongside Resistance Training Can Have More Impacts on Anabolic/Catabolic Hormones, Strength and Body Composition?
research.summary: This study demonstrates that resistance training combined with creatine supplementation significantly increases serum IGF-1 levels, contributing to improved muscle mass and strength.

research.title: Betaine supplement enhances skeletal muscle differentiation in murine myoblasts via IGF-1 signaling activation
research.summary: Research indicates that betaine supplementation improves muscle fiber maturation and hypertrophy by increasing the expression of the IGF-1 receptor and activating its signaling pathways.

research.title: The influence of arginine supplementation on IGF-1: A systematic review and meta-analysis
research.summary: A meta-analysis exploring how arginine intake affects circulating IGF-1 levels, noting its role as a potential secretagogue for the growth hormone/IGF-1 axis.

research.title: Creatine Supplementation and Skeletal Muscle Metabolism for Building Muscle Mass- Review of the Potential Mechanisms of Action
research.summary: This review highlights that creatine increases muscle mass partly by modulating the IGF-1/mTOR pathway, serving as a cellular stressor that triggers protein synthesis.

research.title: The influence of vitamin D supplementation on IGF-1 levels in humans: A systematic review and meta-analysis
research.summary: A large-scale analysis found that Vitamin D supplementation significantly increases circulating IGF-1 concentrations in humans, suggesting a synergistic relationship for bone and muscle health.

sentiment.summary: While users highly value IGF-1 for its powerful muscle-building and recovery effects, there is a strong undercurrent of concern regarding its link to accelerated aging, hair loss, and potential cancer risk.

effects.label: Synergistic Boosters:
effects.description: Supplements like creatine, Vitamin D, and arginine are scientifically noted to naturally elevate circulating IGF-1 levels.

effects.label: Gut Health Connection:
effects.description: Microbial metabolites like short-chain fatty acids (SCFAs) can stimulate liver production of IGF-1.

effects.label: Hair Aging Paradox:
effects.description: High levels of IGF-1 in the skin may paradoxically speed up hair follicle aging and graying despite its growth-promoting reputation.

dosage.label: Natural Support:
dosage.description: Users often stack Vitamin D (2000-5000 IU) and Creatine (3-5g) to maximize endogenous production.

dosage.label: Colostrum Supplementation:
dosage.description: Some users report benefits for gut and muscle health using 400mg to 5g of colostrum daily as a source of growth factors.

dosage.label: Ketogenic Regulation:
dosage.description: For those with excessively high levels, a ketogenic diet or fasting is frequently suggested to lower insulin-driven IGF-1.

sideEffects.label: Dermatological Issues:
sideEffects.description: Elevated IGF-1 is strongly linked to acne breakouts (zits) through the stimulation of sebaceous glands.

sideEffects.label: Cancer Risk:
sideEffects.description: Concerns exist regarding the potential for high IGF-1 levels to accelerate the growth of existing solid malignancies, such as prostate cancer.

sideEffects.label: Insulin Sensitivity:
sideEffects.description: Chronic pharmacological elevation (via HGH or peptides) can negatively impact glucose metabolism and lead to pre-diabetic states.

availability.label: Peptide Market:
availability.description: Users often source IGF-1 or its secretagogues (CJC-1295, Ipamorelin) through 'grey market' research chemical sources due to limited medical prescriptions.

availability.label: Diagnostic Monitoring:
availability.description: Advanced health panels (like Function or Superpower) are commonly used by biohackers to monitor IGF-1 levels because standard doctors may not test it without specific symptoms.
